response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with ~15+ years of experience, but their profile is heavily weighted toward web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the modern C++ expertise this role demands. Their only professional C++ experience was a 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and they show no evidence of IEC 62443/SDL practices, MQTT/IoT protocols, message-broker architectures in C++, or industrial automation domain experience. The low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby (DLang, async I/O, fibers) demonstrates relevant systems-programming aptitude, but the gap to a C++-centric industrial cybersecurity role is too wide for this specific position.
strengths: Deep systems-programming experience with low-latency, high-throughput services (async I/O, caching, fibers)
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, mentoring and workshops
Broad multi-domain experience (web, mobile, desktop, embedded) with event-driven platform design
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (single ~7-month role in 2014-2015); primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js/DLang
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol experience evident
Career trajectory points toward web/full-stack and AI tooling, not embedded/industrial platform engineering

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent C++ and STL depth
